outlook.headline: Lead times are stretching again across most of the supply chain
outlook.summary: The latest mention trend is back up to 1.2x, about 11% above the trailing average, and the company reads are overwhelmingly pointing to lead times lengthening rather than easing. The tightest reads are concentrated in tools, test, distribution, and selected semis—benefiting suppliers with backlog and pricing leverage—while only a few names still describe balanced or weak conditions. Watch whether the current extension broadens further or starts to level off, especially in the more balanced analog and materials pockets.

drivers.label: Broad-based lead-time extension
drivers.detail: Multiple companies said lead times are stretching or extending, signaling demand is running ahead of supply rather than inventory normalization.

drivers.label: Tight supply persists in tools and test
drivers.detail: Equipment and test names continue to describe tight supply and backlog support, suggesting the constraint is not isolated to one node.

drivers.label: Distribution channel remains tighter
drivers.detail: Lead times are gradually increasing across distribution, indicating the channel is still absorbing stronger demand without yet showing shortage-like stress.

drivers.label: Soft pockets remain but are limited
drivers.detail: A few companies still described balanced, ample, or weak conditions, which tempers the breadth of the tightening but does not reverse it.

risks: Lead times flatten at current levels if the 1.2x trend reflects a temporary pull-forward rather than sustained constraint.
Inventory build or stockpiling could mask underlying demand if supply catches up faster than expected.
Balanced/weak pockets at Analog Devices and FormFactor could signal that tightening is not uniform across end markets.
